Petition To Compel Accounting, Administration, And Distrubution Of The Mable W. Henderson Trust, Dated February 2,2005 By Trustee James Henderson;
Off Calendar Jun-16-2008 Continued To Sep-22-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Broitman for James Henderson. Endy Ukoha-Ajike for Cheryl Smith.

Mr. Broitman stated that the accounting had been submitted last week and that the parties agreed to have Mr. Henderson resign. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated that he did not have a chance to review the accounting and that he agreed to have Mr. Henderson resign and to have Cheryl Smith be appointed as Successor Trustee. In addition, he stated that the parties agreed to have all other matters in regard to the accounting mediated, that he had the declinations of Evelyn Backstrom and Martha Ryan and that all the parties had informally agreed to mediation. The Court stated that a stipulation from all parties agreeing to mediate was needed. The Petition to Compel The Accounting is moot. The Petition for Removal Of Trustee is moot. The only matter pending before the court is the distribution of the trust. The matter was continued to September 22, 2008 for status on the mediation. The Court signed the Order Accepting Resignation Of James Henderson As Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Brenda Fields CSR#7235. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.

Petition To Compel Accounting, Administration, And Distrubution Of The Mable W. Henderson Trust, Dated February 2,2005 By Trustee James Henderson;
Off Calendar Apr-28-2008 Continued To Jun-16-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Endy Ukoha-Ajike for Chery Smith, Petitioner; Mr. Charles Jonas for and with James Henderson, Trustee.

The Court questioned about the notice issue to the entitled parties of the Petition that was advanced form May 7, 2008, and no Proof of Service on the Motion for Preliminary Injuction. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated that parties had stipulated on the accounting issues, Mr. Henderson will resign as a Trustee and agreed to the Petition for appointment of a Successor Trustee. Further, he requested for a 90 days continuance for filing of the accounting and for a referral to mediation. Mr. Jonas confirmed with the agreement of the resignation of his client, the accounting and the turn over of the assets of Trust to the Successor Trustee, and also agreed to mediation if there is unresolved accounting issue.
The Court continued the matter to June 16, 2008 for status on filing of the accounting, the Petition to compel accounting and Motion to remove Trustee. The injunction will be taken off calendar on the same day if there are no problems with the turn over. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike to notice all interested parties in regard to both matters today, and to prepare an Order outlining what took place today and for an approval by Mr. Jonas.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris

Motion And Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction, Supporting Declaration Of Petitioner C. Smith, Declaration Of Endy Ukoja-Ajike, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities.
Off Calendar Apr-28-2008 Continued To Jun-16-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Petition To Amend The Temporary Restraining Order Granted On April 10, 2008
Off Calendar Apr-16-2008 Granted Per Written Order

Ex Parte Aplication For An Interim Injunction Preventing Trustee From Utilizing Any Funds
Off Calendar Apr-11-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Endy Ukoha-Ajike for Cheryl Smith (Petitioner). James L. Henderson (Trustee) in Pro Per.

Counsel stated that his client brought this action on behalf of the other beneficiaries, that the decedents settlor passed away on September 3, 2006, and that the Trust was excuted on February 2, 2005. In addition, he stated that distribution was to be done with in 6 months, that real property had been sold in December of 2006 for $700,000.00 and that there were two bank accounts in the trust. Furthermore, he stated that the the Trustee had not contacted the beneficiaries and that nothing had been done in 18 months. Mr. Henderson provided the account numbers for two bank accounts at the Oak Valley Community Bank in Modesto and stated that he purchased a house from an auction last year with the proceeds from the sale of the real property. The Court ordered that Mr. Henderson is prevented from utilizing the funds in the Oak Valley Community Bank accounts #10035285 and #30020457, and that he is not to incumber or sell the real property located at 3929 Ridlon Court, Ceres, Ca., 95307, (signing the order for the same in open court). The hearing on the preliminary injunction will be heard on April 28, 2008. The parties agreed per stipulation in Court that the petition to compel accounting set for May 7, 2008 is advanced and time shortened so that it is also heard on April 28, 2008 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR#8098. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Castillo.
